titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/211

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=477 status=live modified=2021-04-06 16:03:24.841386+00 | translation moa=478 status=live modified=2021-04-06 16:03:23.22088+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte matris ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris Iohannes filius Michaelis de Methkil Salutem . Nouerit vniuersitas uestra me
dedisse concessisse et hac Carta mea confirmasse Deo et Ecclesie Sancte Marie de Melros et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus et ser
uituris totam terram meam de Panneschelys . Tenendam et habendam eisdem monachis de me et heredibus meis in puram et
perpetuam Elemosinam inperpetuum . Ita libere . quiete . plenarie . et honorifice  sicut aliqua elemosina in regno Scotie . Liberius
quietius . et plenius . Tenetur uel possidetur . In Bosco . et plano . In terris cultis et incultis . In . . . in viis et se
mitis . In moris . Mossis . et Maresiis . In aquis . et stagnis . viuariis . et Molendinis . et . . . aisiamentis . que
infra predictam terram inueniri poterunt aut excerceri . Reddendo inde annuatim priori de Melros qui pro tempore fuerit tres
marcas argenti ad pentecostcn . quas pro salute anime mee et heredum meorum assignaui ad emendum pitantiam conueutui de
Melros In die Sancte trinitatis per manum eiusdem prioris annuatim procurandam . Ego autem et heredes mei manutenebimus et defende
mus predictis monachis totam predictam terram cum omnibus libertatibus et iustis pertinenciis suis contra omnes homines . excepta terra quam
dedi Monachis de May . et excepta terra quam concessi Henrico paruo et heredibus suis per rectas diuisas suas in territorio de Panneschelys . Totam uero terram predictam sicut dictum est tenebunt et habebunt prefati Monachi de Melros . . . et quiete
ab omni seruicio consuetudine et exactione et demanda de me et heredibus meis in perpet . . . acien . . .
prenominatum . Hiis testibus . Domino Alano abbate sancti Ewardi de Balmurinach . Thoma Comite de Atholia . W. filio Alani . domini
Regis dapifero . W. Capellano ipsius . Thoma de Cantia . Magistro iohanne Abigensi . Magistro nessio medico domini Regis . Malco
mo filio Comitis de Leuenaus . Gaufrido de Boso . Iacobo filio Willelmi . Iohanne Cumin . Andrea de Suitun’ . et Multis aliis .

p.cl: To all the sons of holy mother Church present and to come, John, son of Michael of Methil sends greeting.

Let your whole community know me to have given, granted, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God and the church of St Mary at Melrose, and the monks in that place who serve God now and in the future, my whole land at Penshiels; for the said monks to have and to hold of me and my heirs in pure and everlasting alms for ever, as freely, quitely, fully and honourably as any alms in the kingdom of Scotland are freely, quitely and fully held and possessed; in wood and plain, in lands cultivated and uncultivated, in . . . , in roads and paths, in moors, peat-bogs, and marshes, in running waters and pools, fish-ponds and mills, and . . . easements which can be found or exercised within the foresaid land; by paying thereupon yearly at Whitsunday to the the one who is prior of Melrose at the time three merks of silver, which for the salvation of my soul and the souls of my heirs I have assigned for purchasing a pittance annually on the day of the Holy Trinity, to the monastic community of Melrose, to be dispensed by the hands of the said prior. And I and my heirs shall maintain and defend to the foresaid monks the whole land aforesaid, with all its liberties and just pertinents, against all men; excepting the lands which I have given to the monks of May, and excepting the land which I have allowed to Henry Little and his heirs, by their correct boundaries, in the territory of [Pen]shiels. But the whole land aforesaid, as it is said, the said monks of Melrose shall hold and have . . . and quit from all service, custom, exaction and demand of me and my heirs for ever, doing as aforesaid.

With these witnesses: the lord Alan, abbot of St Edward's at Balmerino; Thomas, earl of Atholl; Walter, son of Alan, steward of the lord king, W., his chaplain; Thomas of Kent; Master John 'Abigensi'; Mastcr Ness, physician of the lord king; Malcolm, son of the earl of Lennox; Geoffrey del Bois; James, son of William; John Comyn; Andrew of Swinton; and many others.
